ummm. You do know that the oldest know pair of scissors is over 3000 years old. And even if they did not have them. A sharp stone keeps its edge about as long as a stainless steel one. What made them a pain was that they were far heavier and bulkier.  Cloth also existed far longer than most know. What throws you off is what they were made on and how the fashion reflects modern times So while you do have a point. It's not total case of laziness. 

One of the biggest and oldest mistakes made today is thinking that they did not have much in fashion sense.. Well what do you think Roman Togas were? They did not wear those things all the time. Just saying.  As for function. They had to make warm clothes that lasted a long time.  the cold barely dressed caveman is pure Hollywood BS. They had things like leggings/etc...

replies.author: @Fallout-w9z
replies.text: Back in days had fine clothing to future man .

replies.author: @facelessone6555
replies.text:
@Fallout-w9z ;Finer clothing was not unknown in the past. But most of it tended to go towards the rich, the elite. So yeah. The poorer you were. The more you had to work with what you could get. 

Wool clothing was quite common in those days. And I'm NOT talking about sweaters. Wool can be further processed into finer cloth and that's what they did. It's become a bit of a lost art. Oh it can be done but cotton is cheaper these days and we cannot forget the man-made stuff. Much cheaper and much easier to get.

Bottom-line is. It was all about what was worth the time and money in the old days followed by what it was made of. Even leather can be made to be softer if you know how. 

One thing is for sure. Even women had fewer clothes in those days.  A scary thought these days. But true.

replies.author: @sitnamkrad
replies.text:
@Member_zero Sorry to burst your bubble here. While I do agree that swords change the game a little, no actual weapon combat uses *only* the weapon. It's not *that* difficult to grab someone with a sword especially if you have a sword or shield yourself. And yes, I have done it plenty of times, against non-choreographed resisting opponents. 
That said, not with fencing if we're talking the Olympic sport. Those flimsy antennas are nothing like actual weapons. Sure, they might poke a tiny hole somewhere, and you don't want that to be your face, which is why they wear protection. But I guarantee if you swing a sword their way, there's nothing they can do to you to stop it. The weapon is too light to parry it with, and whatever hole they might poke isn't going to have the stopping power required to make your sword stop. Even if we're talking against an unarmed opponent, fencing foils do not have an edge, so they can be grabbed even more easily than normal swords, and they have no structural integrity so once grabbed, they will be bent or broken if the person wants it to. Not to mention even the slightest actual armour like gambeson would be enough to stop it. But perhaps you mean actual rapier fencing?

replies.author: @mauritzvonclare3807
replies.text: Hah! Try studying an actual historical martial art, they all have grappling. If you bind swords, it's actually very easy to close the distance to get into grabbing range. Sure, technique does beat strength in the melee, but if a woman is actually trained to fight, she knows she's at a disadvantage when fighting a man, and prepares appropriately, rather then your silly idea that all her fencing training, which always ends after the first touch, would prepare you at all for a real fight

replies.text:
@Member_zeroIf you’ve never participated in a combat sport. A couple things to note: 
A)There’s a reason weight classes are every 10lbs. 
B)Fighting 1 on 1 is very tiring, that’s why fighters are gassed after 5 minute rounds. 3lb swords get heavy quick.
C) Your most vulnerable point in a fight is when your weapon is embedded in a foe.  If your sword is in my guts I can still stab you too, strength to push and cleave is critical.
D) Reach is extremely important. Taller= more reach
Stronger= longer weapon
E) Blocking with a shield or weapon favors strength. Shields crack and arms get weak

None of this is men vs women. Simply biology. That’s why I thank Mr smith and wess so skinny guys like me have the great equalizer

text:
Comparing this to Xena & Hercules is an insult to these shows.
They were schlock but they were self aware and fun schlock.
Not to mention some really talented people worked on them, like Sam Raimi was the main producer & the costume designers later worked on Peter Jackson's LotR trilogy.
Hell, Anthony Starr ( Homelander) & Karl Urban started their careers there.
Karl Urban even talks very fondly about Xena & Hercules in interviews.
Quote: "People can make fun of these shows as much as they like, but they levelled up the New Zealand movie industry & many people in front & behind the camera, started their careers there, including myself."

author: @khylerbane4523
text: Fun fact, the original version is this was supposed to be a tv show. The production itself was littered with love and fans. Producers, show runners, director and writers were all fans of Sonja and Robert E Howard’s sword and sorcery pulps, even using them as a basis for the stories. But before casting and production proper could start the ones in charged got me too-ed and from there things went down hill. Writing, casting, direction was so changed and had a revolving door of people. One of the actress set to play the She Devil her self was even caught up in all of it. The new heads of the project decided to make Sonja black but not keep her red hair. While the actress was still on board, even learning of Sonja via comics, numerous issues like creative differences… be it things like not changing her hair to red or handling of the characters, or now being a movie not a show… she got fed up and left the project. Even though things got sorted out in the end, everyone knew this was doomed to fail. The actress, who had been reading Red Sonja since childhood and was still determined to play Red Sonja as accurately as age could.

replies.author: @StacieMMeier
replies.text: @Turamwdd Anie mispronounces Conan's name. The correct way to say it is what Howard claimed like Irish, which Conan would be in Howard's world (He really wished it to be Scottish Highlands). Another issue, in Howards stories, Conan is very intelligent. He knew how to speak many languages and a few ancient ones only a few other people knew. He did hunt treasures in old ruins BTW, so he had to know about them and understand the language. Conan also knew how to use magic, though he rarely did it. He did it in the Phoenix on the Sword. And even, in Tower of the Elephant. Conan was an adventurer. a bounty hunter, a treasure seeker, he studied texts, a thief (Sometimes hired), a King, and one of the most feared pirates in Howard's lore. He even was hired to rescue people and helped many.

replies.author: @henrykujawa4427
replies.text:
@c3bhm  When I realized CIRCLE OF IRON was written in the LATE 60s, the whole vibe of the film made a lot of sense. Lee hoped it would co-star James Coburn, but he dropped out and the project fizzled, which may be a big part of why he went to Hong Kong and overnight became a superstar THERE.

replies.author: @StacieMMeier
replies.text:
Even though she isn’t part of the official Conan lore (Howard created her, but set her in the 1600s. He called her Sonya), we don’t know much more about her beyond that short story since he passed before finishing her arc. The Marvel version, while popular, isn’t canon with Conan.

What we do know is that she stood about 183 cm (6'0") and weighed around 72 kg (160 lbs), making her taller and heavier than most men of her era. She wasn’t a tiny figure — her size alone gave her an edge, and combined with her training, survival knowledge, and sheer willpower, her combat abilities make sense. She had to kill efficiently to eat and live, so fighting became second nature.

Of course, she also had the blessing and knowledge of a goddess. While the stories never outright say she was unkillable like the film suggests, the only true way to defeat her would likely have been to remove her head from her body.

text:
Its insane that Arnold has apparently been trying to get an Old King Conan movie off the ground for like the last 8 years or more and yet this movie somehow got made.

It really is a pity though, Red Sonja has very little presence in the mainstream as far as I'm aware and while the 1985 movie wasn't very good, mainly because it was obviously piggybacking off the success of Arnold's Conan, it was a decent framework of what to *avoid* and what worked.

But what I find baffling is they somehow managed to take the character of Red Sonja, who wasn't exactly all that complex, and make her such a nothing burger of a character. I legit can't name a single personality trait of hers in this movie...

replies.author: @GideonRavenor712
replies.text:
This has been explained before in other sources that involve similar outfits for female warrior characters and you people say "It's unrealistic and impractical and just to look sexy":

Putting aside the sex appeal argument, which I won't take seriously unless they want to criticize Conan's physique: Is it wildly impractical? Of course it is . . . if your intention is to take a hit and not get killed.

Many female Dark Eldar in Warhammer fantasy or 40k have similarly skimpy outfits, but the logic behind them wearing them is simple: They're so confident (or arrogant in their case) of their ability to dodge, block or otherwise overpower any attack thrown at them that they don't fear being hit.

Same case here. The running gag of Red Sonja is that she will wed any man who can beat her in a fight . . . and no man has. She wears the chainmail bikini for the same reason, she's confident no opponent will land a serious blow on her.

replies.text:
I don't mind if they cast a short lady for a character like this, but if you are going for a short lady they better be built with a much more powerful frame with some real muscle on it. 
I also don't mind if you are going to cast a toned but tiny stick insect spindly woman like this one all that much, even if its not really the expectation for the character as long as she can actually act - but she really really must be fighting in entirely different ways to make it remotely believable - its got to be all about using that small agile dancers body to evade and wear down the larger opponents looking for an opportunity to make a good strike. The whole really show this little lady has great skill to make up for her lack of size and power, and perhaps if it fits the moment that the big guys overconfidence is their downfall.

replies.text:
@SammaclauseGamgee True, but if you can get the right actor in other ways - the one that really has the talent to express themselves in the body language, facial expression etc in a way that suits the character well you can make it work. Especially if you are willing to break out good direction and camera trickery as Lord of the Rings did to make the hobbits the right scale.

Though in the case of more barbarian characters I agree actor appearance is usually more important than for other characters - stereotypically not very nuanced to allow the actor to carry the role without also packing the size and muscle expected. So its certainly going to be harder to make it work.

replies.author: @foldionepapyrus3441
replies.text:
@SammaclauseGamgee Yeah, though you can get enough of that with good costuming, and in many cases you kinda have to as real human beings just don't tend to look like the highly over exaggerated art usual in comic books. Especially the females, as they are generally well off the bell curve for the gender being either implausibly large and powerful or rather more extremely padded and narrow waisted. Can't say I've ever seen a comic book where the girls look plausible (though I'm not a huge comic book fan either).

They actually did the costuming fairly well here in fairness to them, more than good enough really, its just everything else that is so wrong it doesn't matter - if she moved like a real fighter, had some skill, aggression and style that fits the character and is still wearing the right outfit being smaller would be easy to forgive, and with some camera trickery that she is so much smaller than the character should be can be hidden.

text: Red Sonja's origin story involves a goddess answering her prayer for revenge on the marauders that slaughtered her family, burned down her house, and raped Sonja herself when she was 17. The goddess grants her incredible skill with any weapon, on the condition that she never sleep with a man "unless he defeats her in fair combat." This movie feels like they *almost* gave her that backstory (probably minus the rape given young Sonja's age in the movie), but chickened out at the last minute, cut out the whole resolution of the raiders scene, and then changed nothing else at all.
